questions.question: 中文导读：Why did Mask R-CNN replace RoIPool with RoIAlign?
options: 中文选项：RoIAlign is faster on GPUs

中文选项：RoIPool rounds box coordinates to integers at multiple steps, misaligning the feature map from the input pixels by up to a feature-map pixel; RoIAlign uses bilinear sampling with no rounding, preserving localisation
中文选项：RoIPool only works on CPU
中文选项：RoIPool was a copyright issue
questions.explanation: 中文解析：RoIPool's rounding costs up to a stride-sized misalignment (e.g. 32 pixels on a stride-32 feature map). RoIAlign samples at exact float coordinates via bilinear interpolation. The change lifted mask AP by 3-4 points on COCO in the original paper and is now standard in every detector that cares about localisation.

questions.question: 中文导读：Mask R-CNN's mask head outputs a 28x28 mask per class per proposal. Why per class?
options: 中文选项：Because binary masks need per-class channels for backprop

中文选项：Decoupling mask prediction from classification: the mask head only has to learn the shape for each class separately, so the classifier's decision does not affect which mask is produced, only which channel is read at inference
中文选项：Binary masks overflow in a single channel
中文选项：The paper required it
questions.explanation: 中文解析：Producing one mask per class per proposal decouples mask shape learning from classification. At inference you read only the channel matching the predicted class. This multi-task decoupling matters because mask shape and class probability are different targets that train with different gradients.

questions.question: 中文导读：torchvision's Mask R-CNN prediction dict has `labels` that start at 1, not 0. Why?
options: 中文选项：Legacy bug
中文选项：Class 0 is reserved for background; user classes are always 1-based so the classifier head can treat background as a real class with its own logits and gradients
中文选项：torchvision uses 1-based indexing throughout
中文选项：Training data determined it
questions.explanation: 中文解析：Most detectors treat background as class 0 and explicit foreground classes as 1..C. The softmax over (C+1) classes lets the network learn to actively predict 'no object here' rather than just low confidence on all classes. A dataset with 4 real classes needs num_classes = 5 when swapping the predictor.

questions.question: 中文导读：You fine-tune Mask R-CNN on a 500-image dataset and val mAP plateaus while train loss keeps dropping. What is the first thing to try?
options: 中文选项：Add more epochs
中文选项：Freeze the backbone and FPN so the model only fine-tunes the RPN and heads; 500 images is too few to update 23M backbone parameters without overfitting
中文选项：Switch to segmentation U-Net
中文选项：Use a higher learning rate
questions.explanation: 中文解析：On small datasets you do not have enough signal to fine-tune every parameter. Freezing the pretrained backbone and FPN (ImageNet + COCO features) and training only the RPN objectness head and the two classifier/mask heads is the standard recipe and usually fixes this plateau in one training run.

questions.question: 中文导读：The FPN inside Mask R-CNN has four levels (P2, P3, P4, P5) at strides 4, 8, 16, 32. Why not just use one level?
options: 中文选项：To use more GPU memory

中文选项：Objects in natural images span a range of scales; routing each proposal to the FPN level matching its size gives the head a feature map with the right receptive field for that object, which is strictly better than always using one scale
中文选项：Four levels is arbitrary
中文选项：To reduce parameter count
questions.explanation: 中文解析：A small object on a stride-32 feature map occupies a single cell, which has almost no spatial information. FPN routes small objects to the high-resolution, shallow levels (P2) and large objects to the low-resolution, deep levels (P5), matching receptive field to object size. This is why every modern detector has a pyramid.
